blocks:
Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ عَدُوِّى وَعَدُوَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَآءَ تُلْقُونَ إِلَيْهِم بِٱلْمَوَدَّةِ وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِمَا جَآءَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْحَقِّ يُخْرِجُونَ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۙ أَن تُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ رَبِّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ خَرَجْتُمْ جِهَـٰدًا فِى سَبِيلِى وَٱبْتِغَآءَ مَرْضَاتِى ۚ تُسِرُّونَ إِلَيْهِم بِٱلْمَوَدَّةِ وَأَنَا۠ أَعْلَمُ بِمَآ أَخْفَيْتُمْ وَمَآ أَعْلَنتُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْهُ مِنكُمْ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَوَآءَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (۱)
O you who have faith! Do not take My enemy and your enemy for friends, [secretly] offering them affection, if you have set out for jihad in My way and to seek My pleasure, for they have certainly denied whatever has come to you of the truth, expelling the Apostle and you, because you have faith in Allah, your Lord. You secretly nourish affection for them, while I know well whatever you hide and whatever you disclose, and whoever among you does that has certainly strayed from the right way. (1)

Key Points

The word 'adw (عدّو) is applied both to a single person and to a group of people, but here the intent is a group, because it is used opposite the word awliya (اولیاء), which is plural. (1)

The intent behind the expulsion of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Muslims was the ill-treatment they faced, to the extent that the Prophet and the Muslims left Mecca themselves to escape the mistreatment of the disbelievers. (2)

We read in the commentaries: One of the Prophet's companions صلى الله عليه وآله gave a letter to a woman to take to the Meccan polytheists, informing them of the Prophet's decision to conquer Mecca. Gabriel informed the Messenger of Allah about the matter. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله sent Imam Ali عليه السلام and a group to retrieve the letter from the woman, saying: "You will find that woman in such-and-such an area." The dispatched group moved out and found the woman exactly where the Prophet had indicated. They asked her about the letter. The woman denied knowing about any such letter. Some companions believed the woman and wanted to return to Medina, but Imam Ali عليه السلام said: "Neither Gabriel lied to the Prophet nor did the Prophet lie to us, therefore, the letter in question is definitely with this woman." He then drew his sword to take the letter. Seeing the matter was serious, the woman took the letter out from her hair and handed it over. They brought the letter to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. He summoned the letter's author—Hatib ibn Abi Balta'ah—and reprimanded him. Hatib said: I am not a traitor, but since my relatives live in Mecca among the polytheists, I wanted to win the hearts of the polytheists so that my relatives could live in comfort. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله forgave him, and these verses were revealed.

Interestingly, this woman came from Mecca to Medina and requested help from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. He asked her: Have you embraced Islam? She said: No. He asked: Have you migrated from Mecca to join the Muhajirun (emigrants)? She said: No. He asked: Then why have you come to Medina? She said: To receive aid from you. The Prophet asked: Then what happened to the young men who used to surround you in Mecca? (An allusion that she was a singer in Mecca and had young clients!) She replied: After the defeat of the people of Mecca in the Battle of Badr, no one came to me anymore (an indication of the depth of the defeat's impact on people's morale). The Prophet ordered that she be given aid. (Yes, the command was to help a stranger, even if she was a disbeliever). (3)

This incident shows that preserving military secrets is so essential that Imam Ali عليه السلام threatened to kill the letter carrier, saying: «لاَردنَّ رأسك الى رسول اللّه صلى الله عليه وآله» (4) I will send your head to the Messenger of Allah!

Enemy Recognition in the Quran

The Quran, in numerous verses, reports on the plans and conspiracies of the enemy and reminds Muslims of their duty:

A) Enemy Thoughts and Desires: «لتجدنّ اشدّ النّاس عداوةً للّذین آمنوا الیهود والّذین أشركوا» (5) You will surely find the most intense of people in animosity toward the believers to be the Jews and the polytheists. «ما یودّ الّذین كفروا من أهل الكتاب و لا المشركین أن ینزل علیكم من خیر من ربّكم» (6) Neither those who disbelieve among the People of the Scripture nor the polytheists wish that any good should be sent down to you from your Lord. «ودّوا لو تدهن فیدهنون» (7) They wish that you would compromise so they would compromise. «ودّوا ما عنتم» (8) They wish that you would suffer distress. «ودّ الّذین كفروا لو تغفلون عن أسلحتكم و امتعتكم...» (9) Those who disbelieve wish that you would neglect your weapons and your baggage...

B) Enemy Conspiracies: «انّهم یكیدون كیداً» (10) Indeed, they are planning a plot. «یشترون الضّلالة و یریدون ان تضلّوا السّبیل» (11) They purchase error and wish that you would lose the way.

C) Enemy Behavior: «انّ الكافرین كانوا لكم عدوّاً مبیناً» (12) Indeed, the disbelievers have ever been to you a manifest enemy. «یخادعون اللّه والّذین آمنوا» (13) They deceive Allah and those who believe. «ان یثقفوكم یكونوا لكم اعداء» (14) If they gain dominance over you, they will be your enemies. «آمنوا بالّذى انزل على الّذین آمنوا وجه النهار واكفروا آخره لعلّهم یرجعون» (15) Believe in what was revealed to the believers at the beginning of the day and disbelieve at its end (so that the believers might also doubt the truth of the religion and) return.

D) Duty of Muslims towards the Enemy: «هم العدوّ فاحذرهم» (16) They are the enemy, so beware of them. «واعدّوا لهم ما استطعتم من قوّة...» (17) Prepare against them whatever you are able of power... «لا تتّخذوا بطانة من دونكم لایألونكم خبالاً» (18) Do not take intimates besides yourselves; they will not fail to corrupt you.

1) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 2) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 3) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan, Ruh al-Bayan, Safi, Maraghi, Al-Mizan, and Namuneh, with slight differences in narration. 4) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 5) Al-Ma'idah, 82. 6) Al-Baqarah, 105. 7) Al-Qalam, 9. 8) Al-i 'Imran, 118. 9) An-Nisa, 102. 10) At-Tariq, 15. 11) An-Nisa, 44. 12) An-Nisa, 101. 13) Al-Baqarah, 9. 14) Al-Mumtahanah, 2. 15) Al-i 'Imran, 72. 16) Al-Munafiqun, 4. 17) Al-Anfal, 60. 18) Al-i 'Imran, 118.

Messages

  1. The believing community does not have the right to maintain friendly and intimate relations with the enemies of Allah. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا لاتتخذوا عدوّى و عدوّكم أولیاء»
  2. Faith in Allah is incompatible with establishing bonds of friendship with the enemy of Allah. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتتّخذوا عدوّى... أولیاء»
  3. The enemy of Allah is, in reality, also the enemy of the believers. «عدوّى و عدوّكم»
  4. Although the disbelievers are our enemies, the main reason for our cessation of relations with them is their enmity toward Allah. The word 'adwī (My enemy) comes before 'adwukum (your enemy).
  5. Expressing affection and showing friendship toward enemies increases their audacity against the believers. «تلقون الیهم بالمودّة و قد كفروا بما جاءكم من الحقّ»
  6. State your commands and prohibitions with justification. «لاتتخذوا... قد كفروا... یخرجون الرسول و ایّاكم»
  7. In foreign policy, establishing and severing relations must be based on religious criteria. «لاتتخذوا عدوّى و عدوّكم أولیاء»
  8. Religion is not separate from politics. (The command to sever ties with enemies is a political instruction found within the text of the Quran). «لاتتخذوا عدوّى و عدوّكم أولیاء»
  9. Disbelievers are not merely opposed to you in belief; they are determined to eliminate you. «كفروا... یخرجون الرّسول و ایّاكم»
  10. The enmity of the disbelievers toward you is because of your faith in Allah, your Lord. «أن تؤمنوا باللّه ربّكم»
  11. Establishing relations with disbelievers is permissible only if they respect your values and are not plotting against you. «لاتتخذوا... قد كفروا... یخرجون الرّسول»
  12. Islam desires the dignity of Muslims. (Expressing affection toward a group that disbelieves in your doctrine and plots against you is humiliation). «تلقون الیهم بالمودّة و قد كفروا بما جاءكم من الحق»
  13. One heart cannot hold two loves. «ان كنتم جهاداً فى سبیلى وابتغاء مرضاتى تسرون الیهم بالمودّة»
  14. Jihad must be sincere and for the sake of Allah. «جهاداً فى سبیلى وابتغاء مرضاتى»
  15. Allah is aware of secret communications with disbelievers. «أنا أعلم بما أخفیتم»
  16. Paying attention to Allah's knowledge leads to a reduction in sins. «أنا أعلم بما أخفیتم»
  17. In education, there must be both command and prohibition, «لاتتخذوا» both justification, «عدوى و عدوكم» both motivation, «یخرجون الرسول و ایّاكم» and both threat. «و أنا أعلم بما أخفیتم»
  18. How many believers there are who end up with a bad fate. «الّذین آمنوا... ضلّ سواء السّبیل»
  19. How many individuals there are who were initially fighters (in the way of Allah) but ended up with a bad fate due to relations with enemies. «خرجتم جهاداً فى سبیلى و ابتغاء مرضاتى... ضلّ سواء السّبیل»
  20. The idea of securing benefits through secret relations with the enemies of religion is straying from the path and an action without fruition. «و من یفعله منكم فقد ضلّ سواء السّبیل»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Mumtahanah

This Surah was revealed in Medina and contains thirteen verses. It is named Al-Mumtahanah (The Tested Woman) because of the tenth verse, which concerns the testing of immigrant women. The content of this Surah is about love for Allah the Almighty and the prohibition of friendship with His enemies. It advises Muslims to take Prophet Abraham عليه السلام as their role model and example.

Among the one hundred and fourteen Surahs of the Holy Quran, nine Surahs begin by addressing the issue of enemy recognition: Surahs Bara'ah, Al-Ahzab, Muhammad, Al-Mumtahanah, Al-Munafiqun, Al-Ma'arij, Al-Bayyinah, Al-Kafirun, and Al-Masad. In the Quran, the verses concerning enemy recognition are more numerous than the verses concerning rulings (Ayat al-Ahkam). The word ḍalla (ضَلَّ - strayed) appears 191 times, the word kufr (كفر - disbelief) 521 times, the word nifaq (نفاق - hypocrisy) 37 times, and the word ṣadd (صدّ - hindering) 45 times.

Ayah

إِن يَثْقَفُوكُمْ يَكُونُوا۟ لَكُمْ أَعْدَآءً وَيَبْسُطُوٓا۟ إِلَيْكُمْ أَيْدِيَهُمْ وَأَلْسِنَتَهُم بِٱلسُّوٓءِ وَوَدُّوا۟ لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ (۲)
If they were to confront you they would be your enemies, and would stretch out against you their hands and [unleash] their tongues with evil [intentions], and they are eager that you [too] should be faithless. (2)

Key Points

Thaqafa (ثقف) means to find and to confront. Imam Ali عليه السلام writes in Letter 53 of Nahj al-Balagha, addressing Malik al-Ashtar: "ولكن احذر كلّ الحذر من عدوّك بعد صلحه فانّ العدوّ ربّما قارب لیتغفّل" (But beware, beware of your enemy after peace has been made, for the enemy may approach you only to catch you unawares). Even after peace, beware of the enemy's deceit, because the enemy sometimes approaches you to take you by surprise. And in Ghurar al-Hikam, we read from him (Imam Ali): "لاتأمن عدّواً و ان شكر" (Do not feel secure from an enemy, even if he expresses gratitude). Do not feel secure or safe from an enemy, even if he opens his mouth in praise. The enemy is like water, which, although heated by warmth, eventually cools down. "... فانّه كالماء و ان اطیل سخانه بالنّار لم یمتنع من اطفائه" (For he is like water; even if its heating by fire is prolonged, it will not refrain from cooling down) (19).

There are five reasons for severing ties with the disbelievers (Kuffar): A) They do not accept your school of thought. "كفروا بما جاءكم من الحق" B) They cannot tolerate the Leader (the Prophet) and the community. "یخرجون الرسول و ایّاكم" C) If they gain dominance, they will implement their enmity. "ان یثقفوكم..." D) They harm you with their hands and tongues. "یبسطوا الیكم أیدیهم و ألسنتهم" E) They wish that you would abandon the religion and become apostates. "ودّوا لو تكفرون"

19) Ghurar al-Hikam.

Messages

1- Enemies seek comprehensive dominance over you. "یثقفوكم"

2- The enemy's silence is not a sign of his friendship; rather, it means he has not found an opportunity to strike. "ان یثقفوكم یكونوا لكم اعداءً"

3- The enemy employs both military aggression, "یبسطوا الیكم أیدیهم," and cultural aggression. "و السنتهم"

4- The root of the enemy's aggression with hands and tongues lies in his inner, heartfelt desires. "یبسطوا الیكم أیدیهم و ألسنتكم... ودّوا لو تكفرون"

5- Enemies are united in their hostility toward you. "یكونوا... یبسطوا... ودّوا"

6- The enemy's goal in military and cultural strikes is for you to abandon your school of thought (religion). "ودّوا لو تكفرون"

7- Unless you commit disbelief (Kufr), the enemy will not be satisfied with you. "ودّوا لو تكفرون" (We also read in Ayah 217 of Surah Al-Baqarah: "لایزالون یقاتلونكم حتّى یردّوكم عن دینكم ان استطاعوا")

Ayah

لَن تَنفَعَكُمْ أَرْحَامُكُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ ۚ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۳)
Your relatives and children will not avail you on the Day of Resurrection: He will separate you [from one another], and Allah watches what you do. (3)

Key Points

Since one of the factors for establishing relations with enemies is maintaining family and kinship ties, the Quran states: Your disbelieving family and relatives will be of no use to you on the Day of Resurrection, so do not cast yourselves into sin for their sake. The ineffectiveness of relatives and the severing of means (of connection) on the Day of Resurrection have been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran: «فلاانساب بینهم»(20) On that Day there will be no relationship among them. «تقطّعت بهم الاسباب»(21) On that Day the means (of connection) will be severed.

20) Mu'minun, 101. 21) Baqarah, 166.

Messages

1- Kinship affections sometimes provide the ground for inclination towards enemies. «تسرّون الیهم بالمودة... لن تنفعكم ارحامكم»

2- What motivation do you have for establishing friendly relations with the disbelievers? In their hearts, they wish that you would also disbelieve. «ودّوا لو تكفرون» Their hands and tongues are against you. «یبسطوا الیكم ایدیهم و السنتهم» And they will be of no benefit to you on the Day of Resurrection. «لن تنفعكم»

3- Remember the ineffectiveness of supports and your solitude on the Day of Resurrection so that you cut off hope from the disbelievers, even if they are your relatives. «لن تنفعكم... یوم القیامة»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will separate the misguided relatives from the believers, so do not attach yourselves to them for the sake of a few days of this world. «أولیاء... یفصل بینكم»

5- Belief in Allah's knowledge and supervision is a deterrent against sin. «و اللّه بما تعملون بصیر»

Ayah

قَدْ كَانَتْ لَكُمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ فِىٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥٓ إِذْ قَالُوا۟ لِقَوْمِهِمْ إِنَّا بُرَءَٰٓؤُا۟ مِنكُمْ وَمِمَّا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ كَفَرْنَا بِكُمْ وَبَدَا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمُ ٱلْعَدَٰوَةُ وَٱلْبَغْضَآءُ أَبَدًا حَتَّىٰ تُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَحْدَهُۥٓ إِلَّا قَوْلَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ لَأَسْتَغْفِرَنَّ لَكَ وَمَآ أَمْلِكُ لَكَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۖ رَّبَّنَا عَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْنَا وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْنَا وَإِلَيْكَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (٤)
There is certainly a good exemplar for you in Abraham and those who were with him, when they said to their own people, ‘Indeed we repudiate you and whatever you worship besides Allah. We disown you, and enmity and hate have appeared between you and us for ever, unless you come to have faith in Allah alone,’ apart from Abraham’s saying to his father, ‘I will surely plead forgiveness for you, though I cannot avail you anything against Allah.’ ‘Our Lord! In You do we put our trust, and to You do we turn penitently, and toward You is the destination. (4)

Key Points

"Uswah" (أسوة) derived from "ta'assī" (تأسّى) means following an example or taking someone as a role model. Considering the previous verses, where some Muslims secretly or openly maintained friendship and affection with the disbelievers (kuffār) in order to protect their relatives, this verse states: Learn dissociation (barā'ah) from the disbelievers and polytheists (mushrikūn) from Prophet Abraham (Ibrāhīm) and his companions. When Prophet Abraham saw his father's persistence in disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) and despaired of guiding him, he dissociated himself from him. His seeking forgiveness (istighfār) for him was a promise he had made to his father to prepare the ground for his return. As stated in Surah At-Tawbah, Ayah 114: «و ما كان استغفار ابراهیم لابیه الا عن موعدة وعدها ایّاه فلما تبیّن له انّه عدوّ للّه تبرّأ منه» (And Abraham's seeking forgiveness for his father was only because of a promise he had made to him. But when it became clear to him that he was an enemy of Allah, he dissociated himself from him.)

In the Quran, two men and two women have been introduced as role models (uswah) for the believers: Of the men, one is Prophet Abraham عليه السلام, «قد كانت لكم أسوة حسنة فى ابراهیم» (Indeed, there has been for you an excellent pattern in Abraham), and the other is the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله, «لكم فى رسول اللّه أسوة حسنة» (Indeed, in the Messenger of Allah you have an excellent pattern) (22). And of the women, one is the wife of Pharaoh and the other is Lady Mary (Maryam) (although the followers of Prophet Abraham are also introduced as role models: «قد كانت لكم اسوة فى ابراهیم و الذین معه» [Indeed, there has been for you an excellent pattern in Abraham and those with him], whom the Quran, in Ayahs 11 and 12 of Surah At-Tahrīm, introduces as exemplary women in contrast to the wives of Prophets Noah and Lot, stating: «ضرب اللّه مثلاً للذین آمنوا امرئة فرعون... و مریم ابنت عمران» [Allah presents an example for those who believed: the wife of Pharaoh... and Mary, the daughter of 'Imran]).

Of course, in narrations, other individuals such as Imam Husayn عليه السلام, «فلكم فىّ اسوة» (So, in me you have a role model) (23), and Lady Fatimah (Zahra) عليها السلام have also been introduced as role models. As Imam Mahdi عليه السلام says: «و فى ابنة رسول اللّه لى اسوة حسنة» (24). The daughter of the Messenger of Allah is an excellent role model for me.

Characteristics of Prophet Abraham عليه السلام in the Quran: 1. Complete success in severe Divine tests. «فاتمّهنّ» (25) 2. Service to the Mosque. «طهرّا بیتى» (26) 3. Being submissive to God. «حنیفاً مسلماً» (27) 4. Turning to God and being patient and forbearing (halīm). «انّ ابراهیم لاوّاه حلیم» (28) 5. He was like an entire nation (Ummah). «انّ ابراهیم كان امّة» (29) 6. He was faithful/loyal. «ابراهیم الّذى وفّى » (30) 7. He was brave and heroic. «تاللّه لاكیدنّ أصنامكم» (31). By Allah! I will surely plan against your idols. 8. Migration (Hijrah). «انّى مهاجر الى ربّى» (32) 9. Having self-sacrifice and devotion. «فلمّا أسلما و تلّه للجبین» (33)

22) Ahzāb, 21. 23) Maqtal Abī Makhnaf, p. 86. 24) Bihār, Vol. 53, p. 180. 25) Baqarah, 124. 26) Baqarah, 125. 27) Āl 'Imrān, 67. 28) Tawbah, 114. 29) Nahl, 120. 30) Najm, 37. 31) Anbiyā', 57. 32) 'Ankabūt, 26. 33) Sāffāt, 103.

Messages

1- In education and propagation (da'wah), presenting a tangible, practical example and role model is necessary. «قد كانت لكم أسوة حسنة»

2- Both the Prophets and their followers and those trained by those great figures are role models. «أسوة حسنة فى ابراهیم و الّذین معه»

3- The passage of time does not diminish the role of religious and spiritual role models. Prophet Abraham can be a role model for us today. «كانت لكم أسوة حسنة فى ابراهیم»

4- The misguided relatives of role models are not a reason to abandon those role models. (In the Quran, the word "uswah" is used for the Prophet of Islam and Prophet Abraham, even though the uncle of both was disobedient/misguided).

5- Some role models are excellent (hasanah) and some are ugly/bad. «أسوة حسنة»

6- Keeping pace, accompanying, and maintaining intellectual and practical harmony with Divine leaders is necessary. «و الّذین معه»

7- Firmness and clarity in declaring dissociation (barā'ah) from the polytheists is a value. «اذ قالوا لقومهم انّا بُرءآؤا منكم»

8- Tribal ties and social relationships should not prevent the declaration of truth. «قالوا لقومهم...»

9- The axis of dissociation is polytheism (shirk), the means of polytheism, and distance from God. «برءآؤا منكم و ممّا تعبدون من دون اللّه»

10- Dissociation must be ideological and purposeful, not vengeful. «بدا بیننا و بینكم العداوة والبغضاء ابداً حتّى تؤمنوا»

11- Dissociation is not only verbal, «انّا بُرءآؤا», but has a verbal stage, «كفرنا», a heartfelt stage, and a practical stage, «بدا بیننا و بینكم العداوة والبغضاء».

12- Faith (Imān) must be pure from all forms of polytheism. «تؤمنوا باللّه وحده»

13- Taking a stand and showing decisiveness against disbelief (kufr) and polytheism requires reliance (tawakkul), supplication, and attention to the Hereafter (Ma'ād). «انّا برءآؤا... علیك توكّلنا»

14- The meaning of reliance (tawakkul) is not isolation and passivity; rather, reliance is meaningful alongside the explicit declaration of dissociation and a strong stance against the polytheists. «انّا برءآؤا... كفرنا... توكّلنا»

15- Reliance (tawakkul) is the remedy for removing worries. Since declaring dissociation, especially with decisiveness and clarity, may entail negative consequences, reliance is necessary. «انّا برءآؤا منكم... علیك توكّلنا»

16- Wherever you speak or show resolve, restrain your soul with the remembrance of God. «انّا برءآؤا... كفرنا بكم، بدا بیننا و بینكم... ربّنا علیك توكّلنا، و الیك انبنا، و الیك المصیر»

17- Reliance (tawakkul) and turning (inābah) must be solely to God. «علیك توكّلنا و الیك انبنا» (The words 'alayka [علیك] and ilayka [الیك] preceding tawakkalnā [توكّلنا] and anabnā [انبنا] indicate exclusivity).

18- The friends of God (Awliyā') do not consider themselves owners of anything and do not permit themselves to change Divine law. «لا ستغفرّن لك و ما أملك لك من اللّه من شى ء»

19- The path of all existence and our path is toward Him. «الیك انبنا و الیك المصیر»

Ayah

رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا رَبَّنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٥)
Our Lord! Do not make us a test for the faithless, and forgive us. Our Lord! Indeed You are the All-mighty, the All-wise.’ (5)

Key Points

The word fitnah (فتنة) linguistically means turmoil, test (examination), torment, affliction (calamity), and harassment. The harassment of Muslims is a test for the disbelievers, so that they may reveal their malice and obstinacy, become arrogant due to their dominance, and consider their power a sign of their righteousness (truthfulness). In the previous verses, the discussion was about dissociation (from disbelievers), decisiveness, and avoiding friendship with the disbelievers. This verse, in the form of a supplication (du'a), emphasizes the content of the previous verses, [praying] that O Lord, we may not become tools in the hands of the opponents.

Messages

1- Among the etiquettes of supplication (du'a) is the address "Yā Rabb" (O Lord). Usually, the supplications in the Quran begin with the word "Rabbanā" (Our Lord).

2- Muslims must not act in a way, in all matters—even in economic, military, and cultural agreements—that allows them to be exploited by the disbelievers or opens the door for the disbelievers' pressure and dominance upon them. «لاتجعلنا فتنة للذین كفروا»

3- Religion and politics stand side by side: dissociation from the disbelievers alongside seeking forgiveness from Allah. One supplication has a political dimension, and the other has a spiritual dimension. «ربّنا لاتجعلنا... و اغفرلنا»

4- We should turn to the One who is both powerful and Wise. «ربّنا... انّك أنت العزیز الحكیم»

5- Not becoming a tool in the hands of the disbelievers is the secret to honor (Izzah). «لاتجعلنا فتنةً... أنت العزیز»

6- The behavior of many powerful individuals is not wise, but Allah is both the Invincible Powerful One and the Wise. «أنت العزیز الحكیم»

Ayah

لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِيهِمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ لِّمَن كَانَ يَرْجُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ (٦)
There is certainly a good exemplar for you in them—for those who look forward to Allah and the Last Day—and anyone who refuses to comply [should know that] indeed Allah is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable. (6)

Key Points

In this Ayah, and in Ayah 21 of Surah Al-Ahzab, where the discussion is about the exemplary nature (being a role model) of Prophet Ibrahim and Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, having hope in Allah and the Day of Judgment is introduced as a condition for accepting the role model. «لمن كان یرجوا...» After the word «ربّنا» (Our Lord) was repeated three times in Ayahs 4 and 5, [Allah] states again: they are an excellent example (uswah), meaning they are exemplary not only in disavowal (of polytheism/disbelievers) but also in supplication (du'a) and intimate prayer (munajat).

Messages

1- Divine role models do not have an expiration date. «لقد كان لكم فیهم أسوة حسنة»

2- Not only the Prophets, but also their sincere companions are role models for us. «فیهم»

3- Correct beliefs are the foundation for adopting role models in behavior and ethics. «أسوة لمن كان یرجوا اللّه و الیوم الآخر» (Indeed, those who benefit from correct role models are those whose hearts are illuminated by the light of hope. «یرجوا» And those who turn away are the despairing and, so to speak, the disconnected. «یتولّ»)

4- Faith that is constant is valuable. «كان یرجوا اللّه...» (The combination of kāna [كان] with the imperfect verb indicates continuity and constancy.)

5- The turning away of people from divine role models does not harm Allah or His Awliya (saints/friends). «و من یتولّ فان اللّه هو الغنى الحمید»

Ayah

۞ عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ ٱلَّذِينَ عَادَيْتُم مِّنْهُم مَّوَدَّةً ۚ وَٱللَّهُ قَدِيرٌ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۷)
It may be that Allah will bring about affection between you and those with whom you are at enmity, and Allah is all-powerful, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (7)

Key Points

This Ayah gives a message to the Muslims, who felt a lack and emotional void due to cutting ties with their disbelieving relatives, that this will be compensated for in the future, and many of them will incline toward Islam. Considering that the previous enmity was based on dissociation (barā'ah) from disbelief (kufr), then its conversion into affection (mawaddah) must be through the disbelievers embracing faith, not by the obligation of dissociation being waived.

Messages

1- Do not despair of the guidance of the disbelievers. «عسى اللّه»

2- Enmity is not the principle; rather, it is an incidental necessity, and we must normalize the conditions. «ان یجعل بینكم... مودّة»

3- The transformation of hearts and the creation of affections are in the hands of Allah. «عسى اللّه ان یجعل... مودّة»

4- You should cease secret friendship and affection with the disbelievers; Allah will replace it with true affection. «تلقون الیهم بالمودة... یجعل بینكم... مودّة»

5- Do not be surprised by the conversion of enmity into affection, for Allah is capable of all things. «واللّه قدیر»

6- The past misdeeds of the disbelievers toward the Muslims are forgivable and pardonable. «و اللّه غفور رحیم»

7- Allah's forgiveness stems from His mercy. «و اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

لَّا يَنْهَىٰكُمُ ٱللَّهُ عَنِ ٱلَّذِينَ لَمْ يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ فِى ٱلدِّينِ وَلَمْ يُخْرِجُوكُم مِّن دِيَـٰرِكُمْ أَن تَبَرُّوهُمْ وَتُقْسِطُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُقْسِطِينَ (۸)
Allah does not forbid you from dealing with kindness and justice with those [polytheists] who did not make war against you on account of religion and did not expel you from your homes. Indeed Allah loves the just. (8)

Key Points

Disbelievers are of two types. One group seeks enmity and plots against Muslims. Regarding them, the previous verses stated: Why do you show friendship towards them? Disavow them, for Allah is sufficient for you. However, one group of disbelievers has not taken action against you and is not plotting against you. This verse states: Treat them kindly, and there is no prohibition against establishing relations with them. The foreign policy of Islam is based on attracting and winning over others, and this can be achieved through the following methods: 1. Kindness. «تبروّهم» (Be kind to them). 2. Justice. «تقسطوا الیهم» (Deal justly with them). 3. Time for investigation. «و ان احد من المشركین استجارك فاجره حتّى یسمع كلام اللّه»(34) If one of them asks for a respite, grant him a respite until he hears the Word of Allah. 4. Accepting peace proposals. «و ان جنحوا للسلم فاجنح لها»(35) If they incline towards peaceful coexistence, accept their proposal for peace. 5. Financial aid. «والمؤلّفة قلوبهم»(36) A share of Zakat should be spent on creating affection (or reconciliation) with them. 6. Returning their trusts, fulfilling covenants with them, respecting their treaties, and being kind to their parents.

Based on this verse, non-belligerent disbelievers who do not intend to wage war against Muslims and who live under Islamic rule are entitled to social rights and justice.

(34) Tawbah, 6. (35) Anfal, 61. (36) Tawbah, 60.

Messages

  1. The account of harmless disbelievers is separate from that of harmful disbelievers. «لم یقاتلوكم... لم یخرجوكم»
  2. Islam is a religion of kindness and justice, even towards disbelievers. «ان تبروّهم و تقسطوا الیهم»
  3. Raise motivations by declaring Divine love. «ان اللّه یحبّ المقسطین»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يَنْهَىٰكُمُ ٱللَّهُ عَنِ ٱلَّذِينَ قَـٰتَلُوكُمْ فِى ٱلدِّينِ وَأَخْرَجُوكُم مِّن دِيَـٰرِكُمْ وَظَـٰهَرُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ إِخْرَاجِكُمْ أَن تَوَلَّوْهُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُمْ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۹)
Allah forbids you only in regard to those who made war against you on account of religion and expelled you from your homes and supported [the polytheists of Makkah] in your expulsion, that you make friends with them, and whoever makes friends with them—it is they who are the wrongdoers. (9)

Key Points

Today, as I write the commentary on this Ayah, Israel is attacking Muslim-inhabited areas from land and air, expelling them from their homeland and martyring a group of people. According to the command of this Ayah, it is obligatory upon all Muslims to sever ties with the Zionist regime and not take them as friends. This Ayah is also among the political verses of the Quran. Although the concept of this Ayah was contained within the previous Ayah, Allah presented the issue separately to clarify relationships.

Messages

1- Oppression is forbidden, whether committed independently or through support and participation. (Yes, the supporters of warring disbelievers are partners in their crime.) «اخرجوكم... ظاهروا على اخراجكم»

2- Even if you are oppressed, sever friendship with the disbelievers, but do not cease doing good. (The prohibition is against friendship, not anything else). «ینهاكم... ان تولّوهم»

3- Aggression against the lands of others and expelling people from their homeland is forbidden, and one may fight these oppressors as an Islamic Jihad. «انما ینهاكم عن الّذین... اخرجوكم من دیاركم»

4- Under the guise of philanthropy and humanism, peaceful relations with warring disbelievers are forbidden. «و من یتولهم فاولئك هم الظالمون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا جَآءَكُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتُ مُهَـٰجِرَٰتٍ فَٱمْتَحِنُوهُنَّ ۖ ٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمَـٰنِهِنَّ ۖ فَإِنْ عَلِمْتُمُوهُنَّ مُؤْمِنَـٰتٍ فَلَا تَرْجِعُوهُنَّ إِلَى ٱلْكُفَّارِ ۖ لَا هُنَّ حِلٌّ لَّهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحِلُّونَ لَهُنَّ ۖ وَءَاتُوهُم مَّآ أَنفَقُوا۟ ۚ وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَن تَنكِحُوهُنَّ إِذَآ ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ ۚ وَلَا تُمْسِكُوا۟ بِعِصَمِ ٱلْكَوَافِرِ وَسْـَٔلُوا۟ مَآ أَنفَقْتُمْ وَلْيَسْـَٔلُوا۟ مَآ أَنفَقُوا۟ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ حُكْمُ ٱللَّهِ ۖ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱٠)
O you who have faith! When faithful women come to you as immigrants, test them. Allah knows best [the state of] their faith. Then, if you ascertain them to be [genuinely] faithful women, do not send them back to the faithless. They are not lawful for them, nor are they lawful for them, but give them what [dowry] they have spent [for them]. There is no sin upon you in marrying them when you have given them their dowries. Do not hold on to [conjugal] ties with faithless women. Demand [from the infidels] what you have spent [as dowries], and let the faithless demand [from you] what they have spent [as dowries]. That is Allah’s judgment; He judges between you, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (10)

Key Points

One of the clauses of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was that if a polytheist from Mecca joined the Muslims in Medina, the Muslims should return him to the polytheists. This Ayah states that the issue of return does not apply to immigrant women who have sought refuge with the Muslims. This Ayah is among the legal and social verses of the Quran. The dowry (Mahr) of a disbelieving woman who has become Muslim and joins the Islamic society must be given to her disbelieving husband, "آتوهم ما انفقوا" (give them what they have spent), and this dowry must be paid from the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal).(37) The word "عصم" ('usum) is the plural of "عصمت" ('ismah), meaning a means of preservation or protection. In this Ayah, the justice-nurturing aspect of the school of Islam is manifested: Firstly, it does not abandon the immigrant woman to her own devices; secondly, it does not ignore the right of the disbelieving husband who has lost his wife, and returns the dowry he paid to him; thirdly, it fulfills the woman's instinctive and emotional needs with the command "تنحكوهنّ" (that you marry them) and her financial needs with the command "اجورهنّ" (their dowries). Migration might be due to fleeing the husband, desire for a new life, espionage, or seeking wealth and family; therefore, the motives must be clarified through examination. Disbelievers are of several types:(38) Either they all insist on disbelief, which was addressed by the first Ayah concerning the disavowal and separation of the Muslims: "لاتتخذوا عدوّى و عدوكم أولیاء... قد كفروا بما جاءكم من الحق" (Do not take My enemy and your enemy as allies... they have disbelieved in what has come to you of the truth). Or they collectively abandon hostility toward you: "عسى اللّه ان یجعل بینكم و بین الّذین عادیتم منهم مودّة" (Perhaps Allah will put affection between you and those with whom you have been hostile) (Ayah 7 of this same Surah). Or a separation occurs, for example, the woman becomes Muslim while the man remains a disbeliever, or vice versa, in which case the Muslim joins you: "جاءكم المؤمنات مهاجرات" (The believing women come to you as emigrants). In describing the first dowry, the word "نفقه" (spending/expenditure) is used: "انفقوا... انفقتم" (they spent... you spent), but regarding the dowry for the second marriage, the word "أجورهنّ" (their wages/rewards) is used. Considering the five instances where the word "اجورهنّ" appears in the Quran, it seems that the word "أجر" (wage/reward) is used for widows and their second marriages. When the conversion to Islam of one spouse invalidates the previous contract, then a fortiori (by way of priority), if the woman is a disbeliever before the contract, no contract is established. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: "لاینبغى نكاح اهل الكتاب" (It is not proper to marry the People of the Book). Then the Imam عليه السلام recited this part of the Ayah:(39) "و لاتمسكوا بعصم الكوافر" (and do not hold on to the marriage ties of disbelieving women).

(37) Tafsirs Al-Mizan, Namuneh, Furqan. (38) Tafsirs Kabir Fakhr Razi and Maraghi. (39) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Women are not subordinate to their husbands in choosing their religion or performing their religious duties, and they can even emigrate to preserve their faith. "جاءكم المؤمنات مهاجرات"

2- Those who claim to have returned from disbelief to faith must be tested and examined. "جاءكم المؤمنات... فامتحنوهُنّ"

3- Wherever there is a risk of espionage or similar dangers, selection and examination are necessary. "فامتحنوهُنّ"

4- Look at people with a positive view. "جاءكم المؤمنات مهاجرات فامتحنوهنّ" (Even though they have not yet been tested, the Ayah has given them the title of believer).

5- Allah's knowledge of affairs does not relieve us of responsibility. "فامتحنوهُنّ اللّه اعلم بایمانهنّ"

6- In dealing with individuals, we are commanded to rely on apparent criteria, not on excessive scrupulousness or suspicion. "فامتحنوهنّ اللّه اعلم بایمانهنّ"

7- The role of faith in family and marital issues is very important. If a disbelieving woman becomes a believer, do not return her to her disbelieving husband, because a disbelieving spouse makes the family environment unhealthy. "فان علمتموهنّ مؤمنات فلاترجعوهنّ"

8- The dominance and governance of a disbeliever over a Muslim is forbidden in every respect, whether political and social, about which the Quran says: "لن یجعل اللّه للكافرین على المؤمنین سبیلاً"(40) (Allah will never grant the disbelievers a way [to prevail] over the believers), or familial, about which this Ayah says: a Muslim woman should not be under the command of a disbelieving husband. "فلاترجعوهنّ الى الكفّار"

9- Society and environment affect the individual. The environment of disbelief might absorb the Muslim woman; therefore, do not return her to the disbelievers. "فلاترجعوهنّ الى الكفّار"

10- The disbelief of one party in a marriage leads to an automatic divorce (faskh). "فلاترجعوهنّ الى الكفّار"

11- Do not be satisfied with the man's personal affection for his wife; the husband might love the woman, but the corrupt society might pressure the woman's soul. "فلاترجعوهن الى الكفّار" (and He did not say: "الى زوجه" [to her husband]).

12- Faith is the foundation of marriage, and a disbeliever is not the match (kufw) or equal of a believer. "لا هنّ حلُّ لهم ولا هم یحلّون لهنّ"

13- Marital separation is not a reason to disregard financial rights; even if the disbeliever has paid an expense, it must be paid back to him by the Muslims. "لا هم یحلّون لهنّ و آتوهم ما انفقوا"

14- Every marriage has a separate dowry. (Pay the previous dowry that the disbelieving husband gave, and for the second marriage that you undertake, you must give a separate dowry.) "آتوهم ما انفقوا... اتیتموهنّ اجورهنّ"

15- The right of ownership and the financial rights of non-belligerent disbelievers are preserved. "آتوهم ما انفقوا"

16- The first need of a woman is a worthy husband. "لاجناح علیكم ان تنكحوهنّ"

17- A history of disbelief does not prevent marriage to a new Muslim convert. "لاجناح علیكم أن تنكحوهنّ"

18- The dowry is the right of the woman. "اُجورهنّ"

19- Both the instinctive need must be met, "تنكحوهنّ" (that you marry them), and the financial need. "اُجورهنّ" (their dowries).

20- Do not exploit the womanhood, loneliness, foreignness, or recent conversion to Islam of others. "آتیتموهنّ اُجورهنّ"

21- Disbelief is the key to separation, and issues such as marriage, love for a spouse and children, and history of life together do not prevent separation. "ولاتمسكوا بعصم الكوافر"

22- The preservation of rights is reciprocal; your rights and the rights of the disbelievers must not be ignored. (If a Muslim woman becomes a disbeliever and joins the disbelievers, take back the dowry you spent from the disbelievers; and if a disbelieving woman joins the Muslims, give the dowry that the disbeliever paid to him). "واسئلوا ما أنفقتم ولیسئلوا ما أنفقوا"

23- Accepting refugees is sanctioned in Islam, provided that: firstly, the individuals are tested; secondly, financial issues or beauty and ugliness are not factors in granting refuge; and thirdly, the expenses that the disbelievers have paid are reimbursed to them, and the disbelievers have the right to demand this. "اذا جاءكم... مهاجرات فامتحنوهنّ... آتوهم ما أنفقوا... ولیسئلوا ما أنفقوا"

24- Divine commands are knowledgeable and wise. "ذلكم حكم اللّه یحكم بینكم واللّه علیم حكیم"

(40) Nisa, 141.

Ayah

وَإِن فَاتَكُمْ شَىْءٌ مِّنْ أَزْوَٰجِكُمْ إِلَى ٱلْكُفَّارِ فَعَاقَبْتُمْ فَـَٔاتُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ذَهَبَتْ أَزْوَٰجُهُم مِّثْلَ مَآ أَنفَقُوا۟ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنتُم بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۱)
If anything [of the dowries] pertaining to your wives is not reclaimed from the faithless and then you have your turn, then give to those whose wives have left the like of what they have spent, and be wary of Allah in whom you have faith. (11)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about paying the rights of disbelieving husbands; in this Ayah, the discussion is about taking back one's own rights from them.

Messages

1- Rights must reach their rightful owner, even if delayed. «فاتكم شى ء... فآتوا... مثل»

2- Whatever a Muslim individual loses due to their faith must be compensated by the Islamic system. «فاتكم شى ء... فآتوا»

3- Pursuing the disbelievers to protect the wealth of Muslims and compensate their losses is permissible. «فاتكم... فعاقبتم»

4- Rise up to take your rights. «فاتكم... فعاقبتم»

5- Vulnerable individuals must be supported, and their losses must be compensated from the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal) or the spoils of war (Ghanā'im). «فاتكم شى ء... فآتوا... مثل ما انفقوا»

6- In taking one's rights, piety (taqwa) and self-control are necessary. «مثل ما أنفقوا واتقوا اللّه»

7- Financial matters are a slippery slope; piety and fear of God are necessary. «فآتوا... مثل ما أنفقوا واتقوا اللّه»

8- Both the injured party must observe piety in claiming compensation, not demanding more than what they lost, and the community must not evade paying it. «اتقوا اللّه»

9- The sign of true faith is piety (Taqwa). «واتقوا اللّه الّذى أنتم به مؤمنون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِذَا جَآءَكَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتُ يُبَايِعْنَكَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن لَّا يُشْرِكْنَ بِٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَسْرِقْنَ وَلَا يَزْنِينَ وَلَا يَقْتُلْنَ أَوْلَـٰدَهُنَّ وَلَا يَأْتِينَ بِبُهْتَـٰنٍ يَفْتَرِينَهُۥ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِنَّ وَأَرْجُلِهِنَّ وَلَا يَعْصِينَكَ فِى مَعْرُوفٍ ۙ فَبَايِعْهُنَّ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُنَّ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۲)
O Prophet! If faithful women come to you, to take the oath of allegiance to you, [pledging] that they shall not ascribe any partners to Allah, that they shall not steal, nor commit adultery, nor kill their children, nor utter any slander that they may have intentionally fabricated, nor disobey you in what is right, then accept their allegiance, and plead for them to Allah for forgiveness. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful (12)

Key Points

On the Day of the Conquest of Mecca (Fath Makkah), when the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله took the pledge (Bay'ah) from the men on Mount Safa, the women also came to Him to give their pledge. This Ayah was revealed and explained the manner of the pledge. The Prophet's pledge with the women was such that He placed His blessed hand in a vessel of water and then told the women: "For the pledge, immerse your hands in the water." «ادخلن ایدیكنّ فى هذه الماء فهى البیعة»(41) The conditions mentioned in this Ayah are among the fundamental principles of belief and morality that both men and women must observe.

(41) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Woman is an independent being, possessing free will, the power of choice, and independent legal and real personality, to the extent that in early Islam, women directly conversed and expressed opinions with the Prophet of Islam. «یا أیّها النّبى اذا جاءك المؤمنات یبایعنك...»

2- The approach of the Islamic leader towards men and women is no different, and He is accessible to all. «جاءك المؤمنات»

3- Religion is not separate from politics. «المؤمنات یبایعنك» (The pledge (Bay'ah) is a political matter.)

4- The inclination towards increasing the number of Muslims should not make you neglect values; do not accept just anyone. «یبایعنك على ان لایشركن...»

5- Security is among the issues addressed by the Islamic system:

Financial and economic security, «لایسرقن»

Family security, «لایزنین»

Life security, «لایقتلن»

Reputational and honor security, «لایأتین بهتان»

And political and social security. «لایعصینك فى معروف»

6- Rectifying beliefs precedes rectifying behavior. (First «لایشركن», then other matters.)

7- Faith alone is not sufficient; commitment and a pledge to act and obey the leader of the Islamic society are necessary. «المؤمنات یبایعنك على...»

8- The soundness of women from corruption is the basis for the soundness of society. «لایشركن...»

9- Shirk (polytheism) is unacceptable in any way. (The word «شیئاً» next to «لایشركن» indicates that not even an atom of it is forgivable.)

10- In enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), fight all manifestations of evil: ideological «لایشركن», economic «لایسرقن», sexual «لایزنین», familial «لایقتلن اولادهنّ», and social. «لایأتین بهتان»

11- Preserving people's honor is important, and it should not be damaged even slightly. «لایأتین ببهتانٍ»

12- In the Islamic system, leadership is central, and all people must obey the leader. «لایعصینك» (instead of «لایعصین اللّه»)

13- If evils are absent, traversing the path of good (Ma'ruf) is easy. (Evils are listed separately, but good is mentioned in a single phrase.) «لایعصینك فى معروف»

14- Women stand alongside men in matters of belief, social affairs, morality, and politics, and the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله treated them justly in light of revelation. «فبایعهنّ»

15- The conditions of the pledge must be clear and commensurate with the vulnerabilities of those giving the pledge. (Issues like theft, adultery, infanticide, and slander were more prevalent in the families of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah)). «یبایعنك على أن... فبایعهنّ»

16- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to pray and seek forgiveness (Istighfar) for those who pledge allegiance to Him. «واستغفر لهنّ»

17- Seeking intercession (Tawassul) through the friends of Allah is permissible, and their prayers are answered; otherwise, Allah would not have issued the command to pray. «واستغفر لهنّ»

18- Although embracing Islam covers and wipes out the deeds of the period of disbelief (Kufr), the prayer of the Prophet is still needed. «واستغفر لهنّ»

19- People's bad past records should not prevent praying for them. «استغفر لهنّ»

20- Allah's Mercy and Forgiveness are the support for the answering of prayers. «واستغفر... انّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَوَلَّوْا۟ قَوْمًا غَضِبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ قَدْ يَئِسُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ كَمَا يَئِسَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْقُبُورِ (۱۳)
O you who have faith! Do not befriend a people at whom Allah is wrathful: they have despaired of the Hereafter, just as the faithless have despaired of the occupants of the graves. (13)

Key Points

"Tawallī" (تولّى) sometimes means friendship (love) and sometimes means accepting guardianship (wilāyah) and authority. Both meanings are prohibited in this Ayah.

The intent behind "those who incurred wrath" (al-maghḍūbīn) in this Ayah is the Jews, and the intent behind "the disbelievers" (al-kuffār) is the polytheists of Mecca. The Jews are despairing of the reward of the Hereafter, and the polytheists who deny the Resurrection are despairing of their dead. (42)

The first Ayah of this Surah was concerning dissociation (barā’ah) from the enemies of Allah and the Muslims, and the last Ayah is also about the same matter. This is the only Surah whose first and last Ayahs begin with "يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا" (O you who have believed).

Those Who Incurred Wrath in the Quran

In the Quran, individuals and groups have incurred Divine wrath, including:

Concerning the Jews, He says: "و باؤا بغضب" (and they incurred wrath) (43). They were afflicted by the might and wrath of Allah.

Concerning murderers and hypocrites, He says: "غضب اللّه علیهم" (Allah was wroth with them) (44). Allah was wroth with them.

Concerning scholars who do not act upon their knowledge, He says: "كبر مقتاً عند اللّه" (great is hatred in the sight of Allah) (45). Great wrath is reserved by Allah for those who do not act upon their words and promises.

Individuals such as Qarun, Pharaoh, Abu Lahab, and Bal'am Ba'ura have also incurred wrath in the Quran.

Despair of the Hereafter means despair of Allah’s Power, Wisdom, and Justice. Whoever does not accept the Hereafter reaches a dead end. He has no reason for existence or persistence. For he says to himself: If, after long periods of suffering and effort, we are to be annihilated by death, it is better to annihilate ourselves starting today.

If everyone is heading toward annihilation, then education, training, and service have no meaning. If this building is destined to be blown up, then constructing, protecting, repairing, and decorating it have no meaning.

If we are to be sacrificed and perish, why should we forgo pleasures and desires?

Since we will not be questioned, let us sacrifice people for our own desires while we are alive. 42) Tafsir al-Mizan. 43) Aal-i-Imran, 112. 44) Al-Fath, 6. 45) Ghafir, 35.

Messages

1- The friend of Allah does not maintain a relationship of friendship with those who incur His wrath. "يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَوَلَّوْا"

2- Although the People of the Book believe in the Resurrection, because they have no hope in the reward of the Hereafter, they incur the wrath of Allah. Their situation on the Day of Resurrection is no different from that of the disbelievers who deny the Return. "يَئِسُوا مِنَ الْآخِرَةِ كَمَا يَئِسَ الْكُفَّارُ"
